_doc: AssetForge v2 export manifest. Deterministic: `npm run assets` renders every entry here from design-system/masters/ to exact pixel sizes. Reproducible (byte-identical on re-run).

layered._doc: H1 rim: rotating group alone (spin sprite) + full master with the rotating group removed (static base). Engine rotates h1_spin over h1_base.
H2 gauge: needle group alone + face with the needle removed. Engine rotates gauge_needle over gauge_base by meter value.

Brand mark inner disc (Motion Polish v2): the five-fold bpair blade cluster (one real <g id=bpair> plus four <use> clones at 72/144/216/288deg) alone on transparency (spin sprite) + the static rim/hub base with those blades removed. Engine rotates brand_mark_spin continuously over brand_mark_base as the loading-screen loader, per DESIGN_SYSTEM's brand-mark law.

plates_json.note: Symbol id to signature colour for engine edge tinting of the tile plate. H1 and wild are cyan/magenta duos (primary listed); scatter is spectrum (gold anchor).

particles.note: ANIMATION UPLIFT PASS 2026-07-16. Reused across the splash's slow-rotating outer ring, the bonus-entry title card's shockwave, and win banners' expanding ring - CSS filter: hue-rotate() retints per context rather than shipping multiple colour variants.
ANIMATION UPLIFT PASS 2026-07-16. Win-banner particle bursts, anticipation edge sparks, idle-attract symbol glints.
ANIMATION UPLIFT PASS 2026-07-16. Win-banner coin fountain (epic/max tiers).
ANIMATION UPLIFT PASS 2026-07-16. Bonus-entry flame-jet ignition wisps. Grey-blue sourced from the existing fs-knob chrome gradient token (docs/design/CHROME_PRIMITIVES.md), not a new colour.

brand_exports.note: ANIMATION UPLIFT PASS 2026-07-16, item 1 (splash). Palette-compressed bundle copy of the locked studio master (design-system/brand/hero_emblem/master_512.png, ratified PR #82) - ~124KB vs the 417KB master, no visible quality loss at splash scale. The design-system master itself is never modified by this step. HeroSplash.svelte matches its own backdrop colour to the master's own background (#080a16, measured during ingest) rather than chroma-keying, since alpha transparency roughly doubled file size for a soft-edge gradient without shrinking it.

storefront_tile._doc: JOB 7 scaffold (2026-07-14, output slots only, no art). Per docs/stake-engine-live/game-tile-requirements.md, the published spec gives format/naming/file-size rules but NO pixel dimensions or text-safe-area guidance - the w/h values below are Claude's own provisional defaults, not a Stake-published number; confirm against the dashboard Tile Editor before Fable finalises art. NOT read by build.py's main() (which only loops symbols/exports/layered/tile_plate/plates_json by name) - inert until a future pass wires it in and the src masters exist.

tile_background.note: NEW ART REQUIRED - environmental background showing the world of the game. No existing master fits (the in-game background is a video asset, not tile-appropriate). Combined with tile_foreground_hero must not exceed 3MB total per the published requirement.

tile_foreground_hero.note: NEW ART REQUIRED, but scene_character_car.svg (the existing in-game identity-character master, racer + hover car, currently exported 1200x656 for in-scene HUD placement) is a strong reference/starting point for Fable rather than a from-scratch design - the tile hero likely needs an isolated re-crop/re-composition on transparency.

provider_logo.format: PNG, transparent background, high resolution, legible at small sizes
provider_logo.note: NO NEW ART NEEDED - brand_mark.svg is already the WRS provider logo (design-system/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md) and already exports at 512x512 (ui/brand_mark.png) and 192x192 (ui/brand_mark_glyph.png), both transparent. This slot re-points the existing master at a dedicated storefront output path; confirm with Fable/owner before treating as final.
